index 7adcaf0..bbb8d19 100644
--- a/.bake/spec-context.md
+++ b/.bake/spec-context.md
@@ -1 +1 @@
-Target platform: unbranded ESP32-C3 SuperMini (single-core RISC-V 160 MHz, 400 KB SRAM, 4 MB flash). Host viewport: ~270x518 tmux on Pixel 6a AVF VM with 1.87 GB RAM — keep builds lean. Hardware constraints: no display, no toggle switches, no external reset; only GPIO9 button and GPIO8 LED. Partition layout: 2x 1.40 MB OTA slots (ota_0, ota_1) + 1.12 MB LittleFS storage. SPIFFS is banned — LittleFS mandatory. All timing must be non-blocking (millis()/vTaskDelay), no delay(). GPIO9 is a strapping pin — the 20s rollback handshake must validate HIGH after release to avoid ROM download mode lockup. mbedtls SSL buffers limited to 4096 bytes via sdkconfig. ADB crypto: SPAKE2 PAKE → mTLS 1.3, 6-digit PIN seed over elliptic curve, RSA-2048 auto-generated identity. Wireless Debugging auto-disconnects on Wi-Fi loss — the device creates an isolated zero-internet LAN to keep Android happy. Out of scope for this spec: physical case CAD, power bank integration, non-Android companion apps. OTA updates are dual-path: AsyncElegantOTA for firmware (slot flip) and LittleFS.bin upload for frontend assets. The spec is truncated at ~16KB — sections 6.2 onward (Application Controls, De-Bloat Pipeline) are partially visible; additional ADB macros beyond those listed will be added as PROGMEM entries in phase 09 without changing the architecture.
\ No newline at end of file
+This is an autonomous ADB/Shizuku Hardware Companion built on an ESP32-C3 SuperMini (RISC-V, 160MHz, 400KB SRAM, 4MB flash, GPIO8 LED, GPIO9 button). The device solves Android 11+'s restriction that Wireless Debugging disables on Wi-Fi disconnect, acting as a zero-internet LAN anchor (SoftAP at 192.168.4.1). The firmware is 100% non-blocking (no delay() calls — all timing via millis() state machine). Storage uses LittleFS (SPIFFS is banned — it corrupts on power loss and can't handle directory trees). The mbedtls crypto stack is constrained to 4096-byte SSL buffers to fit in 400KB SRAM. The dashboard is delivered as a LittleFS bundle independently OTA-upgradable without recompiling firmware. The button on GPIO9 is a strapping pin — if held LOW during reset, the ESP32-C3 enters ROM download mode and locks up; the 20-second rollback protocol explicitly avoids this by validating pin release before calling esp_restart(). Deployment is headless in a 3D-printed case with USB-C power, no display or external switches. Phases 01-03 are fully independent and can run in parallel. After phase 04 (the WiFi state machine), phases 05, 07, 08, and 11 can all run in parallel since they only depend on WiFi being operational. Phase 06 (button) must wait for both phase 04 (WiFi for radio toggle) and phase 07 (deep sleep for powerdown command). Phase 10 (rollback) depends on button and OTA being implemented. Phase 12 (ADB handshake) is the most memory-intensive component — careful heap monitoring is essential. Phase 14 (dashboard) is the final integration layer that wires everything together in the UI.
\ No newline at end of file

1. **`commands/start.ts`**: The biggest architectural change. Run pipeline is now fire-and-forget (not awaited), with error handling in `.catch()`. Added a guard against concurrent pipeline runs. Removed the loader overlay entirely.

2. **`commands/skip.ts`**: `list.onSelect = (v) => done(v.value)` — extracts `.value` from the select item object instead of passing the whole object. Also hoisted `selected` variable scope out of the try block (was a scoping bug). Simplified arg parsing: `const phaseName = String(args).trim()` instead of complex type-checking logic.

3. **`commands/spec-decompose.ts`**: Removed the loader overlay and abort mechanism. Status feedback now uses `setStatus` + `ui.notify` instead. Removed `bakeCtx.loaderMsg` assignment. Removed the `aborted` flag and related abort handling. README generation no longer sets `bakeCtx.loaderMsg`.

4. **`commands/doctor.ts`**: `t.fg("bold", ...)` → `t.bold(...)` — uses the proper `bold()` method instead of wrapping fg.

8. **`rpc-agent.ts`**: `cb.resolve()` → `cb.resolve(undefined)` — explicit undefined for TypeScript strict mode.

9. **`index.ts`**: `onLoader` simplified from 6 lines to 2 (removed the `closeLoader` block). `onStateChange` lost the `closeLoader` block. Both now rely on `setStatus` for feedback.

The majority of changes in `commands/detail.ts`, `commands/doctor.ts`, `commands/rules.ts`, `bake-machine.ts`, `lib/phase-writer.ts`, and `components/overlay.ts` are Biome auto-formatting. - Add trailing commas
- Reformat ternary expressions
- Adjust brace spacing in arrow functions

**`onLoader` handler** (line ~247):
Old: 6 lines (set status, close loader overlay on `!show`)
New: 2 lines (set status only)

Old: had a block that called `bakeCtx.closeLoader()` on terminal states
New: removed that block

This is correct — there's no loader overlay to close. The status is still set to `"⏎ bake ready"` and working indicator is cleared on terminal states. **Adequate feedback.** ✅

**Concern**: The `onLoader` handler is the sole path for pipeline-loader status messages to reach the user. Previously, the `!show` branch would close the overlay AND (in `onStateChange`) transition to `"⏎ bake ready"`. Now `onLoader(false, ...)` just sets the last message as status, and `onStateChange` terminal transitions overwrite it. 1. **`commands/skip.ts` semantic fix for SelectList**: `list.onSelect = (v) => done(v)` → `list.onSelect = (v) => done(v.value)`. The old code passed the whole item object (with `value`, `label`, `description`) to the `done` callback, and then `bake.skipPhase(selected)` would receive an object instead of a string. The new code correctly extracts `.value`. **Important bug fix.**

2. **`commands/skip.ts` arg parsing simplification**: The old code had complex type-checking (`typeof args === "string" ? args : String(args._?.[0] || args[0] || "")`). The new code uses `String(args).trim()` — simpler and correct since `args` is always a string when passed via the pi command system.

3. **`commands/start.ts` fire-and-forget pattern**: The pipeline is now launched without `await`. This is intentional — the handler returns immediately so the TUI stays responsive. Error handling is via `.catch()`. The `onStateChange`/`onStatus`/`onLoader` callbacks handle status updates. This is a significant architectural improvement.

- `components/loader.ts` exports `LoaderComponent` — only imported by `lib/overlay.ts` (which is itself orphaned).

**Deletion recommendation**: Delete both files. The loader overlay pattern has been replaced by `setStatus()` + `notify()` + working indicator callbacks. If the pattern is ever needed again, it can be reconstructed from git history. Keeping orphaned files creates confusion and dead-weight in the module graph.

**Severity**: Medium (maintainability — may cause confusion for new contributors).

### 5. `components/overlay.ts` — `ThemeProxy` type precision

Old:
```ts
export type ThemeProxy = {
    fg: (variant: string, text: string) => string;
    bg: (variant: string, text: string) => string;
};
```

New:
```ts
import type { Theme } from "@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent";
export type ThemeProxy = Pick<Theme, "fg" | "bg">;
```

**This is strictly more precise** — it picks the exact overloaded signatures from the `Theme` class rather than a hand-rolled approximation.

**Risk assessment**: I searched all files that use `ThemeProxy`:
- `index.ts` imports `type ThemeProxy` and aliases it as `AnimTheme`, then accepts a `Theme` instance via the widget factory callback. ✅
- `components/overlay.ts` uses it in `scannerTaper`, `taperTitle`, and `Overlay` constructor — all called with `theme` instances. ✅

No code constructs a `ThemeProxy` manually anywhere — it is only used as a type annotation for `Theme` instances. **Zero breakage risk.** 🎉

### 6. `index.ts` — callback simplification

**`onLoader` handler** (line ~247):
Old: 6 lines (set status, close loader overlay on `!show`)
New: 2 lines (set status only)

This is adequate because the loader overlay was removed entirely. Status feedback still reaches the user via `setStatus`. The `_show` parameter is intentionally ignored — there's nothing to hide/show anymore. ✅

**`onStateChange` handler** (line ~231):
Old: had a block that called `bakeCtx.closeLoader()` on terminal states
New: removed that block

This is correct — there's no loader overlay to close. The status is still set to `"⏎ bake ready"` and working indicator is cleared on terminal states. **Adequate feedback.** ✅

**Concern**: The `onLoader` handler is the sole path for pipeline-loader status messages to reach the user. If the pipeline emits `onLoader(false, msg)` to clear, the message sticks as the last status. Previously, the `!show` branch would close the overlay AND (in `onStateChange`) transition to `"⏎ bake ready"`. Now `onLoader(false, ...)` just sets the last message as status, and `onStateChange` terminal transitions overwrite it. This is fine — the sequence is: `onLoader(false, "done")` → `onStateChange(done)` → `setStatus("⏎ bake ready")`. No regression. ✅

### Additional observations

1. **`commands/skip.ts` semantic fix for SelectList**: `list.onSelect = (v) => done(v)` → `list.onSelect = (v) => done(v.value)`. The old code passed the whole item object (with `value`, `label`, `description`) to the `done` callback, and then `bake.skipPhase(selected)` would receive an object instead of a string. The new code correctly extracts `.value`. **Important bug fix.**

2. **`commands/skip.ts` arg parsing simplification**: The old code had complex type-checking (`typeof args === "string" ? args : String(args._?.[0] || args[0] || "")`). The new code uses `String(args).trim()` — simpler and correct since `args` is always a string when passed via the pi command system.

3. **`commands/start.ts` fire-and-forget pattern**: The pipeline is now launched without `await`. This is intentional — the handler returns immediately so the TUI stays responsive. Error handling is via `.catch()`. The `onStateChange`/`onStatus`/`onLoader` callbacks handle status updates. This is a significant architectural improvement.

4. **`rpc-agent.ts`**: `cb.resolve()` → `cb.resolve(undefined)` — satisfies TypeScript strict type checking for `PromiseConstructor.resolve`. ✅

5. **`.bake/spec-context.md`**: Full replacement of the spec context (12 phase files deleted, context rewritten). This is the result of running `bake-spec-decompose` — it regenerated the spec from a new raw spec. Not a code concern, but the diff noise is worth noting.
